_comment: Authored paraphrases of EU List of Waste (2014/955/EU) descriptions for the CONCEPT eval. Each rewrites the official wording with plain-language synonyms and removes the discriminative keywords, so a model that merely memorised the code<->string mapping cannot keyword-match, while one that understands the concept still maps the paraphrase to the correct code. Codes/descriptions verified against the parsed List of Waste. Pure-chemical entries (named acids/solvents) are excluded because any paraphrase just re-names the chemical.

paraphrases.01 01 01: residues left from digging up metal-bearing ore
paraphrases.01 01 02: leftover material from quarrying rock that holds no metal
paraphrases.01 03 04: ground-rock sludge from treating sulphur-rich ore that turns acidic over time
paraphrases.02 01 03: discarded green plant matter and vegetable tissue
paraphrases.02 01 04: scrap synthetic-polymer material, not counting wrapping or containers
paraphrases.02 01 07: residues produced by logging and woodland upkeep
paraphrases.03 01 01: discarded tree outer-layer strippings and cork offcuts
paraphrases.03 02 01: carbon-based timber-protection chemicals free of chlorine-family elements
paraphrases.03 02 02: timber-preserving chemicals built on chlorine-bearing organic compounds
paraphrases.04 01 01: trimmed animal-hide tissue and lime-treated skin splits from leather making
paraphrases.04 01 03: solid oil-stripping residue that holds solvents but has no free liquid
paraphrases.04 01 04: used hide-curing bath liquid that contains chromium
paraphrases.05 01 02: sediment from the crude-oil salt-removal stage
paraphrases.05 01 03: settled residue at the base of petroleum storage tanks
paraphrases.05 01 04: acidic hydrocarbon-chain sludge from oil refining
paraphrases.07 02 14: spent manufacturing additives that carry dangerous constituents
paraphrases.07 02 16: waste holding silicone compounds classed as dangerous
paraphrases.08 01 11: leftover paint and varnish carrying organic solvents or other dangerous substances
paraphrases.08 01 21: used product for stripping off paint or varnish
paraphrases.09 01 01: spent water-based photographic developer and activator liquids
paraphrases.09 01 02: used water-based developer liquid for offset printing plates
paraphrases.10 01 03: light airborne ash from burning peat and untreated timber
paraphrases.10 01 04: fine ash and boiler dust from burning fuel oil
paraphrases.10 01 05: dry calcium-based residues from scrubbing sulphur out of flue gases
paraphrases.11 01 08: sludge from applying phosphate coatings to metal
paraphrases.11 01 09: sludge and filter-press residue carrying dangerous substances
paraphrases.11 01 11: water-based rinse liquids carrying dangerous substances
paraphrases.12 01 03: shavings and turnings of metals other than iron
paraphrases.13 01 01: fluid for hydraulic systems that contains PCBs
paraphrases.14 06 02: used halogen-bearing cleaning solvents and their blends
paraphrases.14 06 03: used cleaning solvents and blends without halogens
paraphrases.15 01 01: discarded paper and cardboard wrapping and containers
paraphrases.16 01 03: worn-out tyres that have reached the end of their life
paraphrases.16 01 04: vehicles scrapped at the end of their service life
paraphrases.16 01 06: scrapped vehicles drained of fluids and stripped of dangerous parts
paraphrases.17 01 03: discarded ceramic tiles and pottery-type material
paraphrases.17 02 04: glass, plastic and wood contaminated with dangerous substances
paraphrases.17 03 01: asphalt-type road mixtures containing coal tar
paraphrases.18 01 01: medical sharp instruments such as needles
paraphrases.18 01 02: human body parts, organs and blood-storage bags from healthcare
paraphrases.18 01 10: dental filling amalgam waste from tooth care
paraphrases.19 01 02: iron-based materials recovered from furnace bottom ash
paraphrases.19 01 05: filter-residue cake from cleaning waste gases
paraphrases.19 01 06: water-based liquid waste from gas cleaning
paraphrases.20 01 08: compostable food and kitchen scraps from homes and canteens
paraphrases.20 01 21: spent fluorescent light tubes and other mercury-bearing waste
paraphrases.20 01 23: thrown-out appliances that contain CFC refrigerants
